[Ya ma is the name given to 1/8 th of a day]

The word 'Divasa' or 'Dina' means Day. Remaining part
of the above Prasnamarga sloka clarifies this. Same is also
clear from the use 'Dina-Ratra' (Day and night). Whenever
ancient sages had to mention the 24 hour time span they used
the words 'Ahoratra' or 'Vara', both of which mean the same.
Above definition ofYama as 1/8 th of a day is rdatively
new. Ancient definition of Yama was not like this. Prior to
Amara kosa (a Sanskrit dictionary) definition of Yarna was
as 1/8 th of Ahoratra. In that period Y ama is known by one
more name 'Prahara'. Amarakosa says · <:t '41'1REii!1 i!f1ti - the
words Yama and Prahara mean the same.
In ancient past the word Ya ma meant 60/8 = 7.5 Khati (24/
8 = 3 hours). Thus in ancient past the words Ya ma and Prahara
meant 3 hours of time. Half of it was known as Yamardha
